Title: Analysis of the genome Rehmannia chingii identifies RcCYP72H7 as an epoxidase in iridoid glycoside biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal iridoid glycoside with neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, anti-apoptotic, antitumor, antioxidant, and antifibrotic effects
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves, especially tender shoots and veinless leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RcSQM is unlikely to be involved in conversion of aucubin to catalpol; transient expression with aucubin substrate did not produce catalpol
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: candidate gene identification; expression analysis across tissues; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana with aucubin feeding; LC-MS detection of catalpol
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: relatively higher expression in leaf tissues, roots, and corollas, but low overall compared with iridoid-pathway genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: relatively higher expression in leaf tissues, roots, and corollas
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMDC83 overexpression vector; promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: aucubin feeding in transiently infiltrated Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcSQM (Rch08d0024) was the relatively more expressed SQM candidate in R. chingii. 2. Transient expression of RcSQM in N. benthamiana with aucubin feeding failed to produce catalpol. 3. RcSQM was therefore excluded as the aucubin epoxidase candidate for catalpol biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low direct value for catalpol engineering because the study indicates RcSQM is not the target epoxidase; useful mainly as a negative control to avoid mis-targeting in pathway engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper tested the long-standing hypothesis that SE/SQM might catalyze aucubin epoxidation and rejected it experimentally.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: biosynthetic intermediate supplying iridoid glycoside biosynthesis including catalpol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates geraniol at the C-10 position to form 10-hydroxygeraniol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate/precursor for downstream catalpol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key early pathway enzyme; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RcG10H possesses geraniol 10-hydroxylase activity and supports iridoid glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae WAT11; geraniol feeding; HPLC and GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly expressed in leaves, veinless leaves, and leaf veins; low in roots and stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly expressed in leaves, veinless leaves, and leaf veins; low in roots and stems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcG10H clustered with catalpol-related P450 candidates and showed leaf-enriched expression. 2. Yeast expressing RcG10H converted geraniol into a product matching authentic 10-hydroxygeraniol by HPLC and GC-MS. 3. RcG10H is therefore a functional geraniol 10-hydroxylase in the R. chingii iridoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream pathway gene for reconstruction or enhancement of iridoid/catalpol biosynthesis, especially when stacked with downstream iridoid genes such as 10HGO, IS, IO and RcCYP72H7
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper used RcG10H as a validated positive reference among candidate P450s in the pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal iridoid glycoside with neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, anti-apoptotic, antitumor, antioxidant, and antifibrotic effects
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves, especially tender shoots and veinless leaves
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: epoxidizes aucubin at the C7-C8 position to form catalpol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant of catalpol formation; key control step strongly supported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RcCYP72H7 catalyzes aucubin epoxidation to catalpol and its transient expression increases catalpol accumulation in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: candidate screening from CYP72 cluster; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with aucubin feeding; LC-MS/MS product detection; GFP fusion western blot; active GFP fusion assay; transient overexpression in Rehmannia chingii leaves; HPLC quantification; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high expression in leaf, veinless leaf, and leaf vein; lower in roots, stems, root cortex, and root xylem
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: in R. chingii, high in leaf tissues; in transient assays expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMDC83 overexpression vector; 35S promoter used for GFP fusion/localization constructs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: aucubin feeding in Agrobacterium-infiltrated Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; transient expression in R. chingii leaves under 26°C, dark 24 h then 14 h light/12 h dark
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcCYP72H7 is a member of a CYP72A gene cluster on chromosome 8 and is highly expressed in leaf tissues where iridoids accumulate. 2. Among seven CYP72 candidates tested in N. benthamiana with aucubin feeding, only RcCYP72H7 produced a metabolite matching catalpol by retention time and MS/MS. 3. RcCYP72H7-GFP also retained activity, confirming the protein assignment. 4. Transient expression of RcCYP72H7 in R. chingii leaves increased catalpol content by 55.07%. 5. RcCYP72H7 localizes to the endoplasmic reticulum and was designated catalpol synthase (RcCS).
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for catalpol biofortification and medicinal trait improvement in Rehmannia; can be overexpressed or transcriptionally activated in leaf tissues and stacked with upstream iridoid genes and likely aucubin synthases (e.g., CYP72E13/E14) to increase pathway flux; useful for synthetic biology reconstruction of catalpol production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential off-target effects on other CYP72-mediated terpenoid metabolism were not assessed; no growth/yield penalties reported in transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome, RNA-seq, BioProject PRJCA032868; genome assembly GWHFIKC00000000.1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The gene was designated RcCS by the authors. It expands known CYP72 functions by demonstrating epoxidase activity in plant terpenoid metabolism.
